query: How does changing to another bachelor programme work at the University of Vienna?

queries: How does changing to another bachelor programme work at the University of Vienna?

retrieved.title: Change of degree programme
heading_path: General information regarding the change of a degree programme
retrieved.text:
- Number of changes of degree programme: You can change your degree programme as often as you want to, according to the University of Vienna. However, a change of degree programme might impact your family allowance and/or study grant. Please contact the responsible authority (local Tax Office or Austrian Study Grant Authority) if you have any questions in this regard. Clarify the consequences on a potential residence permit with the Immigration Office (MA 35) or the responsible district commission ("Bezirkshauptmannschaft").
- Minimum academic achievement: Since winter semester 2022/23 onwards you have to achieve [at least 16 ECTS](/en/minimum-number-of-credits/) for each bachelor/diploma programme you are admitted to. If this is not the case, the degree programme will be closed.
- Transferring to the University of Vienna from another university: The information regarding the change of degree programmes applies to students admitted at the University of Vienna. Students who wish to transfer to the University of Vienna have to complete the admission procedure. The entry into a higher semester is not possible. After [completing the admission procedure](/en/admission-procedure/) you can apply for potential recognition of your previous examinations at the [StudiesServiceCenter or StudiesServiceUnit](https://ufind.univie.ac.at/en/search.html?filter=all&query=StudienServiceCenter) responsible for your degree programme. Thereby you might shorten the duration of your degree programme.

retrieved.title: Studying at the University of Vienna again – different degree programme
retrieved.text: You were admitted to a bachelor/diploma or master programme at the University of Vienna and want to study a different degree programme? Here you can find detailed information to best prepare your return to the University of Vienna.

retrieved.title: Studying at the University of Vienna again – different degree programme
heading_path: General information about your return to the University of Vienna
retrieved.text: - Minimum academic achievement: Since winter semester 2022/23 you have to achieve [at least 16 ECTS](/en/minimum-number-of-credits/) for each bachelor/diploma programme you are admitted to. If this is not the case, the degree programme will be closed. - Change of name: If your legal name has changed, please disclose this at an early stage. Message us via the [contact form](/en/contact-form/) and attach a scan of your valid passport or personal ID card and a proof of the name change (e.g. marriage certificate or official notice of the name change). - Application and admission period: The deadlines for your admission at the University of Vienna depend on the type of degree programme and your citizenship. You can find detailed information at the respective type of degree programme. - Recognition of exams: The [StudiesServiceCenter (SSC)/StudiesServiceUnit (SSS)](https://www.univie.ac.at/ueber-uns/leitung-organisation/studien-lehrorganisation/) of the respective degree programme can provide information regarding recognition of prior study achievements and help you to organise your studies. - Completion of the admission: You do not have to book an online appointment. - Simultaneous admission at two Austrian universities: It is not permitted to be admitted to the same degree programme at two Austrian universities at the same time. If you have already studied the chosen degree programme at another university, please send your confirmation of withdrawal via the [contact form](/en/contact-form/). - Organising your studies: [Tips and tricks to plan your studies and register

retrieved.title: Additional degree programmes
retrieved.text:
You are currently admitted to a degree programme at the University of Vienna and would like to study an additional degree programme? Your steps in the admission procedure and the application period depend on the type of degree programme.

[## Bachelor/diploma programmes](/en/admission/additional-degree-programmes/additional-bachelordiploma-programme/)

[## Master programmes](/en/admission/additional-degree-programmes/additional-master-programme/)

- Number of degree programmes: According to the regulations of the University of Vienna, you can be admitted to an unlimited number of degree programmes. Please note that studying multiple degree programmes successfully and actively entails a higher effort regarding [study organisation](/en/study-organisation/). All degree programmes offered at the University of Vienna are full-time programmes. There is no distinction between main and secondary degree programme. Please contact the responsible authority (local Tax Office or Austrian Study Grant Authority) if you have any questions regarding family allowance and/or study grants.
- Recognition of exams: The [StudiesServiceCenter (SSC)/StudiesServiceUnit (SSS)](https://www.univie.ac.at/ueber-uns/leitung-organisation/studien-lehrorganisation/) of the respective degree programme can provide information regarding recognition of prior study achievements and help you to organise your studies.
- Minimum academic achievement: Since winter semester 2022/23 onwards you have to achieve [at least 16 ECTS](/en/minimum-number-of-credits/) for each bachelor/diploma programme you are admitted to. If this is not the case, the degree programme will be closed.

- [Information regarding admission to a third teaching subject as complementary degree programme "Erweiterungsstudium drittes Unterrichtsfach" (only available in German)](https://studieren.univie.ac.at/zulassung/lehramtsstudien/erweiterungsstudium/#c277199)

retrieved.title: Resumption of the same degree programme
retrieved.text: You were admitted to a regular bachelor/diploma or master programme at the University of Vienna and want to resume studying the same degree programme? Here you can find detailed information about this topic.

retrieved.title: Studying a non-degree programme (again)
retrieved.text:
You were admitted to the University of Vienna in the past and want to be admitted to a non-degree programme now? Non-degree students are not admitted to a specific degree programme but, for example, to the "attending of individual courses" or to a continuing education programme.

You were admitted at the University of Vienna in the past and want to visit individual courses out of interest? If this is the case, you have to note several things:

- Examination attempts in the non-degree programme are also counted in the degree programme. If all examination attempts are graded negatively, you will be permanently excluded from the degree programme, and you cannot study this degree programme.
- You have to pay the [tuition fee as well as the Students’ Union fee](/en/tuition-fee/).
- Course places are preferably distributed among degree students.
- During a non-degree programme it is not possible to pass (partial) diploma examinations, module examinations or public defenses.

Message us via the [contact form](/en/contact-form/) within the application period and attach a scan of your passport or personal ID card. Please also send us the certificate of your name change, if applicable. We will admit you to the non-degree programme and inform you about further steps via e-mail. The processing may take several days.

Another option is the admission to a bachelor/diploma or master programme, if you fulfil the admission requirements.

retrieved.title: Change of bachelor/diploma programmes
heading_path: Bachelor/diploma programmes with entrance exam procedure
Note: New curriculum from winter semester 2026/27
retrieved.text: - Changing your teaching subjects also constitutes a change of degree programme. This means, from winter semester 2026/27 onwards, changing your teaching subject will automatically result in being transferred to the new curriculum. You do not need to apply separately for the transfer. - If you study in combination with another university, you also have to notify them of any changes. [Information about the new curriculum](https://lehramt.univie.ac.at/umstieg-in-das-neue-lehramtsstudium) (only in German) #### Transcultural Communication: changing the amount of working languages It is possible to change the number of working languages in the bachelor programme Transcultural Communication once. This means that you can switch from studying with two working languages (A, Bx) and extension curricula to studying with three working languages (A, Bx and By) or vice versa only once. Send us your message in which you indicate the desired change via the [Servicedesk](https://servicedesk.univie.ac.at/plugins/servlet/desk/portal/62/create/936) during the admission period (in this case it does not make a difference where your secondary-school leaving certificate is from). Specify the number of working languages and which languages (A, Bx and if applicable By) you choose. The Admission Office will effectuate the change, which you will see after a few days in u:space by clicking on Studies > Study overview. You will also receive a confirmation via e-mail. Please note: The admission to the degree programme with the new working languages is only in effect starting from the

retrieved.title: Studying at the University of Vienna again – different degree programme
heading_path: Different bachelor/diploma programme without entrance exam procedure
retrieved.text: You want to be admitted to a different bachelor/diploma programme without entrance exam procedure? Apply for admission in u:space within the application period, as described in the [information regarding the admission procedure](/en/admission-procedure/bachelordiploma-programmes/). You do not have to book an online appointment to complete the admission procedure. Your further steps depend on the country of issuance of your secondary-school leaving certificate and your citizenship. - Your secondary-school leaving certificate and citizenship are from an EU/EEA country or Switzerland: Message us via the [contact form](/en/contact-form/) within the admission period and attach a scan of your passport or personal ID card. We will implement the admission and inform you about further steps via e-mail. The processing may take several days. - Your secondary-school leaving certificate is from a non-EU/EEA country: Wait for your admission letter. The processing may take several weeks. You will receive your admission letter and information on further steps via e-mail. / - Bachelor/diploma programmes without entrance exam procedure - African Studies - Ancient History and Studies in Classical Antiquity - Astronomy - Byzantine Studies and Modern Greek Studies - Catholic Theology - Classical Archaeology - Classical Philology - Comparative Literature - Earth Sciences - Egyptology - European Ethnology - Geography - German Philology - History - History of Art - Hungarian and Finnish Studies - Islamic Theology - Japanology - Jewish Studies - Languages and Cultures of South Asia

retrieved.title: Information for asylum seekers
heading_path: How do I get admitted to a degree programme?
retrieved.text: How do I get admitted to a degree programme? In order to be admitted to a bachelor/diploma programme at the University of Vienna you are required to possess a secondary-school leaving certificate or to have completed a degree programme of at least three years. Admission to master programmes require the completion of a subject-related bachelor programme. - You need to provide the required documents in their original or legalised form. If you no longer possess your secondary-school leaving certificate or your higher education degree certificate, you may request a substitute confirmation at the [Federal Ministry Women, Science and Research Republic of Austria](https://www.bmfwf.gv.at/public.html). Admission is not possible without a secondary-school leaving certificate or higher education degree certificate. - Documents issued in a language other than German or English additionally require a legally attested translation. Your documents do not require [full diplomatic certification](/en/admission/info-third-country-students/legalisation/), irrespective of the issuing country. However, you need to prove your status as a recognised refugee/asylum seeker (e.g. by means of a convention travel document). You can submit your application for admission online on [u:space](https://uspace.univie.ac.at/en/). This is only possible during the [application period](/en/admission/application-and-admission-periods/). The University of Vienna conducts an [entrance exam procedure](/en/entrance-exam/) for select bachelor/diploma programmes. An entrance exam procedure is also conducted for admission to the bachelor programme in Teacher Education. You can apply for admission to these programmes only once per academic year, in spring. The entrance

The aim of the bilingual diploma supplement (in German/English) is to enhance the transparency and recognition of higher education degrees. Your diploma supplement contains information about the type and level of your degree, the degree programme you completed (requirements, course of studies, grading scale) as well as as standardised information about the higher education system. Related Links: - [u:space](https://uspace.univie.ac.at/en/web/gast/home) Related entries: - [Completion of a degree programme](/en/abc-of-terminology/related-entries/?tx_irfaq_pi1%5BshowUid%5D=30172&cHash=3f411d6175185cfa1b401855807771dd) - [Diploma Supplement](/en/abc-of-terminology/related-entries/?tx_irfaq_pi1%5BshowUid%5D=30852&cHash=e0f7f659728ec124c1ab41f985dc9792) - Change of degree programme Would you like to change your degree programme at the University of Vienna? Apply for admission to the new degree programme in u:space. Please note the application deadlines! Then write a message to the Admission Office via the Service Desk and have your previous degree programme closed. Related Links: - [Information on the change of degree programme](/en/admission/change-of-degree-programme/) - Closing a degree programme/withdrawing from the University of Vienna The closure of a degree programme is not subject to any deadlines at the University of Vienna. Therefore, you can request the closure of a degree programme throughout the year. If you request to close all your degree programmes, this is referred to as “withdrawal from the university”. Related Links: - [Closing a degree programme/withdrawal from university](/en/admission/closing-a-degree-programme/) - [Resumption of studies](/en/admission/resumption-of-studies/) Related entries: - [Confirmation of withdrawal ("Abgangsbescheinigung")](/en/abc-of-terminology/related-entries/?tx_irfaq_pi1%5BshowUid%5D=30169&cHash=4d6b9c8665691a99bc99014b4a29d313) - [Termination of a degree programme](/en/abc-of-terminology/related-entries/?tx_irfaq_pi1%5BshowUid%5D=30831&cHash=b781b61903ed2560155fd624dbf8e70b) - Colloquium A colloquium can

retrieved.title: Change of bachelor/diploma programmes
retrieved.text:
You would like to change your bachelor/diploma programme?

Your steps in the change of degree programme depend on two factors:

1. Does your chosen degree programme have a compulsory Online-Self-Assessment (OSA) or entrance exam procedure?
2. Is your secondary-school leaving certificate and citizenship from an EU/EEA country or Switzerland, or from a non-EU/EEA country?

retrieved.title: I am from ... and want to study at the University of Vienna. What do I have to keep in mind?
heading_path: FAQ
retrieved.text: to pass, before I submit my application for admission? Unfortunately, this is not possible as which and if any supplementary examinations are prescribed will only be determined individually during the processing of your documents. - I already have a university degree and now want to study a bachelor/diploma programme. Can I apply with this certificate instead of my secondary-school leaving certificate? Yes, if you have completed a programme with a duration of at least three years or at least 180 ECTS (e.g. a three-year bachelor programme) at a recognised post-secondary educational institution you can use this as proof of your university entrance qualification instead of your secondary-school leaving certificate.

retrieved.title: Returning to the University of Vienna – Resumption of studies
retrieved.text:
You did study at the University of Vienna in the past and want to start studying again? Your steps in the admission procedure vary, depending on whether you want to be admitted to the same or a different degree programme or rather a non-degree programme.

[## I want to study the same degree programme again.](/en/admission/resumption-of-studies/same-degree-programme/)

[## I want to study a different degree programme.](/en/admission/resumption-of-studies/other-degree-programme/)

[## I want to be admitted to a non-degree programme or attend the VWU again.](/en/admission/resumption-of-studies/non-degree-programme/)

- [Detailed information on the tuition fee/Students' Union fee](/en/tuition-fee/)

retrieved.title: Making up for supplementary examinations from the bachelor programme for admission to a master programme
heading_path: Consequences of a non-degree programme
retrieved.text:
- Examination attempts in the non-degree programme also count for the degree programme. If all examination attempts are graded negatively in the non-degree programme, you are permanently excluded from the degree programme and cannot be admitted to this programme at the University of Vienna.
- You have to pay the [tuition fee as well as the Students' Union fee](/en/tuition-fee/), irrespective of your citizenship.
- You are not admitted to a specific degree programme, but to attending individual courses ("Besuch einzelner Lehrveranstaltungen").
- The admission to a non-degree programme is possibile until the end of the admission period. However, the registration deadlines for courses end before the beginning of the semester in some cases. Therefore, it is possible that you miss these deadlines.
- Students admitted to a degree programme are favoured in the allocation of course places. If you have any questions regarding the registration for courses and examinations, please contact the [StudiesServiceCenter or StudiesServiceUnit](https://www.univie.ac.at/en/about-us/governance-structure/organisation-of-studies-courses/) of the degree programme in which the courses are offered.
- You are only allowed to take individual examinations/courses and must comply with the curriuclum's registration requirements. Furthermore, you are not allowed to take any public defenses. Graduating is not possible.
- There might be restrictions/limitations when it comes to health insurance, residence permit and financial assistance. Please make an inquiry at the responsible authority about the consequences of your non-degree programme up front.

heading_path: T
retrieved.text: in Vienna and Lower Austria](https://lehramt.univie.ac.at/) (in German) Related entries: - [Teacher Education for General Secondary Education in Vienna and Lower Austria](/en/abc-of-terminology/related-entries/?tx_irfaq_pi1%5BshowUid%5D=31331&cHash=a9508ca1d8a392e52ec541141e6afc7d) - Termination of a degree programme You can end your studies in the following ways: - successful graduation: You will receive your graduation documents in the StudyServiceCenter (SSC) of your degree programme (e.g. certificate, diploma supplement) - discontinuing your degree programme: You can print out a confirmation of withdrawal from university via u:space. Related Links: - [Closing degree programmes/withdrawal from University of Vienna](/en/admission/closing-a-degree-programme/) - [Graduations](https://studienpraeses.univie.ac.at/infos-zum-studienrecht/studienabschluesse/) Related entries: - [Completion of a degree programme](/en/abc-of-terminology/related-entries/?tx_irfaq_pi1%5BshowUid%5D=30172&cHash=3f411d6175185cfa1b401855807771dd) - [Closing a degree programme/withdrawing from the University of Vienna](/en/abc-of-terminology/related-entries/?tx_irfaq_pi1%5BshowUid%5D=31896&cHash=2e24f8e1dcdf22ae4e1123db52ef795d) - Tolerance semesters EU/EEA citizens and persons treated as equivalent only pay the Students' Union fee for the presecribed duration of study (= "minimum duration of study"). At the end of this period, they are granted two additional "tolerance semesters", during which only the Studens' Union fee is charged. If you do not complete your degree programme in the prescribed duration of studies + the two tolerance semesters, you will pay both the Students' Union fee and the tuition fee. Related Links: - [Tuition fee/Students' Union fee](/en/tuition-fee/) Related entries: - [Students’ Union fee (ÖH fee)](/en/abc-of-terminology/related-entries/?tx_irfaq_pi1%5BshowUid%5D=31205&cHash=ed830f954f65ec47794ec4809327b5d1) - [Tuition fee](/en/abc-of-terminology/related-entries/?tx_irfaq_pi1%5BshowUid%5D=31268&cHash=5186d8ac34fa30c4b1270fc725ce2ef7) - Transcript of records ("Sammelzeugnis") Your transcript of records lists all grades, positive and negative ones. It is an official proof of your academic progress, which you need

- Number of degree programmes: According to the regulations of the University of Vienna, you can be admitted to an unlimited number of degree programmes. Please note that studying multiple degree programmes successfully and actively entails a higher effort regarding [study organisation](/en/study-organisation/). All degree programmes offered at the University of Vienna are full-time programmes. There is no distinction between main and secondary degree programme. Please contact the responsible authority (local Tax Office or Austrian Study Grant Authority) if you have any questions regarding family allowance and/or study grants.
- Recognition of exams: The [StudiesServiceCenter (SSC)/StudiesServiceUnit (SSS)](https://www.univie.ac.at/ueber-uns/leitung-organisation/studien-lehrorganisation/) of the respective degree programme can provide information regarding recognition of prior study achievements and help you to organise your studies.
- Minimum academic achievement: Since winter semester 2022/23 onwards you have to achieve [at least 16 ECTS](/en/minimum-number-of-credits/) for each bachelor/diploma programme you are admitted to. If this is not the case, the degree programme will be closed.

- [Information regarding admission to a third teaching subject as complementary degree programme "Erweiterungsstudium drittes Unterrichtsfach" (only available in German)](https://studieren.univie.ac.at/zulassung/lehramtsstudien/erweiterungsstudium/#c277199)

reranked.title: Resumption of the same degree programme
reranked.text: You were admitted to a regular bachelor/diploma or master programme at the University of Vienna and want to resume studying the same degree programme? Here you can find detailed information about this topic.

reranked.title: Studying a non-degree programme (again)
reranked.text:
You were admitted to the University of Vienna in the past and want to be admitted to a non-degree programme now? Non-degree students are not admitted to a specific degree programme but, for example, to the "attending of individual courses" or to a continuing education programme.

You were admitted at the University of Vienna in the past and want to visit individual courses out of interest? If this is the case, you have to note several things:

- Examination attempts in the non-degree programme are also counted in the degree programme. If all examination attempts are graded negatively, you will be permanently excluded from the degree programme, and you cannot study this degree programme.
- You have to pay the [tuition fee as well as the Students’ Union fee](/en/tuition-fee/).
- Course places are preferably distributed among degree students.
- During a non-degree programme it is not possible to pass (partial) diploma examinations, module examinations or public defenses.

Message us via the [contact form](/en/contact-form/) within the application period and attach a scan of your passport or personal ID card. Please also send us the certificate of your name change, if applicable. We will admit you to the non-degree programme and inform you about further steps via e-mail. The processing may take several days.

Another option is the admission to a bachelor/diploma or master programme, if you fulfil the admission requirements.

reranked.title: Change of bachelor/diploma programmes
heading_path: Bachelor/diploma programmes with entrance exam procedure
Note: New curriculum from winter semester 2026/27
reranked.text: - Changing your teaching subjects also constitutes a change of degree programme. This means, from winter semester 2026/27 onwards, changing your teaching subject will automatically result in being transferred to the new curriculum. You do not need to apply separately for the transfer. - If you study in combination with another university, you also have to notify them of any changes. [Information about the new curriculum](https://lehramt.univie.ac.at/umstieg-in-das-neue-lehramtsstudium) (only in German) #### Transcultural Communication: changing the amount of working languages It is possible to change the number of working languages in the bachelor programme Transcultural Communication once. This means that you can switch from studying with two working languages (A, Bx) and extension curricula to studying with three working languages (A, Bx and By) or vice versa only once. Send us your message in which you indicate the desired change via the [Servicedesk](https://servicedesk.univie.ac.at/plugins/servlet/desk/portal/62/create/936) during the admission period (in this case it does not make a difference where your secondary-school leaving certificate is from). Specify the number of working languages and which languages (A, Bx and if applicable By) you choose. The Admission Office will effectuate the change, which you will see after a few days in u:space by clicking on Studies > Study overview. You will also receive a confirmation via e-mail. Please note: The admission to the degree programme with the new working languages is only in effect starting from the

reranked.title: Change of degree programme
heading_path: Further information:
reranked.text:
Information regarding the change from a non-degree to a degree programme after:

- [Completing the University Preparation Programme of the Vienna Universities](/en/admission/non-degree-programme/university-preparation-programme-of-the-vienna-universities-vwu/)
- [Receiving the secondary-school leaving certificate "Nachmatura" (only available in German)](https://studieren.univie.ac.at/zulassung/ausserordentliches-studium/nachmatura/)
- [Receiving the "Studienberechtigungszeugnis" (only available in German)](https://studieren.univie.ac.at/zulassung/studienberechtigungspruefung-studieren-ohne-matura/abschluss-der-studienberechtigungspruefung/)

reranked.title: Studying at the University of Vienna again – different degree programme
heading_path: Different bachelor/diploma programme without entrance exam procedure
reranked.text: You want to be admitted to a different bachelor/diploma programme without entrance exam procedure? Apply for admission in u:space within the application period, as described in the [information regarding the admission procedure](/en/admission-procedure/bachelordiploma-programmes/). You do not have to book an online appointment to complete the admission procedure. Your further steps depend on the country of issuance of your secondary-school leaving certificate and your citizenship. - Your secondary-school leaving certificate and citizenship are from an EU/EEA country or Switzerland: Message us via the [contact form](/en/contact-form/) within the admission period and attach a scan of your passport or personal ID card. We will implement the admission and inform you about further steps via e-mail. The processing may take several days. - Your secondary-school leaving certificate is from a non-EU/EEA country: Wait for your admission letter. The processing may take several weeks. You will receive your admission letter and information on further steps via e-mail. / - Bachelor/diploma programmes without entrance exam procedure - African Studies - Ancient History and Studies in Classical Antiquity - Astronomy - Byzantine Studies and Modern Greek Studies - Catholic Theology - Classical Archaeology - Classical Philology - Comparative Literature - Earth Sciences - Egyptology - European Ethnology - Geography - German Philology - History - History of Art - Hungarian and Finnish Studies - Islamic Theology - Japanology - Jewish Studies - Languages and Cultures of South Asia

reranked.title: Studying at the University of Vienna again – different degree programme
heading_path: General information about your return to the University of Vienna
reranked.text: universities at the same time. If you have already studied the chosen degree programme at another university, please send your confirmation of withdrawal via the [contact form](/en/contact-form/). - Organising your studies: [Tips and tricks to plan your studies and register for courses and exams](/en/study-organisation/).

reranked.title: Information for asylum seekers
heading_path: How do I get admitted to a degree programme?
reranked.text: How do I get admitted to a degree programme? In order to be admitted to a bachelor/diploma programme at the University of Vienna you are required to possess a secondary-school leaving certificate or to have completed a degree programme of at least three years. Admission to master programmes require the completion of a subject-related bachelor programme. - You need to provide the required documents in their original or legalised form. If you no longer possess your secondary-school leaving certificate or your higher education degree certificate, you may request a substitute confirmation at the [Federal Ministry Women, Science and Research Republic of Austria](https://www.bmfwf.gv.at/public.html). Admission is not possible without a secondary-school leaving certificate or higher education degree certificate. - Documents issued in a language other than German or English additionally require a legally attested translation. Your documents do not require [full diplomatic certification](/en/admission/info-third-country-students/legalisation/), irrespective of the issuing country. However, you need to prove your status as a recognised refugee/asylum seeker (e.g. by means of a convention travel document). You can submit your application for admission online on [u:space](https://uspace.univie.ac.at/en/). This is only possible during the [application period](/en/admission/application-and-admission-periods/). The University of Vienna conducts an [entrance exam procedure](/en/entrance-exam/) for select bachelor/diploma programmes. An entrance exam procedure is also conducted for admission to the bachelor programme in Teacher Education. You can apply for admission to these programmes only once per academic year, in spring. The entrance

reranked.title: ABC of terminology
heading_path: C
reranked.text: the academic degree. You also receive your diploma supplement. The aim of the bilingual diploma supplement (in German/English) is to enhance the transparency and recognition of higher education degrees. Your diploma supplement contains information about the type and level of your degree, the degree programme you completed (requirements, course of studies, grading scale) as well as as standardised information about the higher education system. Related Links: - [u:space](https://uspace.univie.ac.at/en/web/gast/home) Related entries: - [Completion of a degree programme](/en/abc-of-terminology/related-entries/?tx_irfaq_pi1%5BshowUid%5D=30172&cHash=3f411d6175185cfa1b401855807771dd) - [Diploma Supplement](/en/abc-of-terminology/related-entries/?tx_irfaq_pi1%5BshowUid%5D=30852&cHash=e0f7f659728ec124c1ab41f985dc9792) - Change of degree programme Would you like to change your degree programme at the University of Vienna? Apply for admission to the new degree programme in u:space. Please note the application deadlines! Then write a message to the Admission Office via the Service Desk and have your previous degree programme closed. Related Links: - [Information on the change of degree programme](/en/admission/change-of-degree-programme/) - Closing a degree programme/withdrawing from the University of Vienna The closure of a degree programme is not subject to any deadlines at the University of Vienna. Therefore, you can request the closure of a degree programme throughout the year. If you request to close all your degree programmes, this is referred to as “withdrawal from the university”. Related Links: - [Closing a degree programme/withdrawal from university](/en/admission/closing-a-degree-programme/) - [Resumption of studies](/en/admission/resumption-of-studies/) Related entries: - [Confirmation of withdrawal ("Abgangsbescheinigung")](/en/abc-of-terminology/related-entries/?tx_irfaq_pi1%5BshowUid%5D=30169&cHash=4d6b9c8665691a99bc99014b4a29d313) - [Termination of a degree programme](/en/abc-of-terminology/related-entries/?tx_irfaq_pi1%5BshowUid%5D=30831&cHash=b781b61903ed2560155fd624dbf8e70b) - Colloquium A colloquium can

reranked.title: Change of bachelor/diploma programmes
reranked.text:
You would like to change your bachelor/diploma programme?

Your steps in the change of degree programme depend on two factors:

1. Does your chosen degree programme have a compulsory Online-Self-Assessment (OSA) or entrance exam procedure?
2. Is your secondary-school leaving certificate and citizenship from an EU/EEA country or Switzerland, or from a non-EU/EEA country?

reranked.title: I am from ... and want to study at the University of Vienna. What do I have to keep in mind?
heading_path: FAQ
reranked.text: to pass, before I submit my application for admission? Unfortunately, this is not possible as which and if any supplementary examinations are prescribed will only be determined individually during the processing of your documents. - I already have a university degree and now want to study a bachelor/diploma programme. Can I apply with this certificate instead of my secondary-school leaving certificate? Yes, if you have completed a programme with a duration of at least three years or at least 180 ECTS (e.g. a three-year bachelor programme) at a recognised post-secondary educational institution you can use this as proof of your university entrance qualification instead of your secondary-school leaving certificate.

reranked.title: Returning to the University of Vienna – Resumption of studies
reranked.text:
You did study at the University of Vienna in the past and want to start studying again? Your steps in the admission procedure vary, depending on whether you want to be admitted to the same or a different degree programme or rather a non-degree programme.

[## I want to study the same degree programme again.](/en/admission/resumption-of-studies/same-degree-programme/)

[## I want to study a different degree programme.](/en/admission/resumption-of-studies/other-degree-programme/)

[## I want to be admitted to a non-degree programme or attend the VWU again.](/en/admission/resumption-of-studies/non-degree-programme/)

- [Detailed information on the tuition fee/Students' Union fee](/en/tuition-fee/)

reranked.title: Making up for supplementary examinations from the bachelor programme for admission to a master programme
heading_path: Consequences of a non-degree programme
reranked.text:
- Examination attempts in the non-degree programme also count for the degree programme. If all examination attempts are graded negatively in the non-degree programme, you are permanently excluded from the degree programme and cannot be admitted to this programme at the University of Vienna.
- You have to pay the [tuition fee as well as the Students' Union fee](/en/tuition-fee/), irrespective of your citizenship.
- You are not admitted to a specific degree programme, but to attending individual courses ("Besuch einzelner Lehrveranstaltungen").
- The admission to a non-degree programme is possibile until the end of the admission period. However, the registration deadlines for courses end before the beginning of the semester in some cases. Therefore, it is possible that you miss these deadlines.
- Students admitted to a degree programme are favoured in the allocation of course places. If you have any questions regarding the registration for courses and examinations, please contact the [StudiesServiceCenter or StudiesServiceUnit](https://www.univie.ac.at/en/about-us/governance-structure/organisation-of-studies-courses/) of the degree programme in which the courses are offered.
- You are only allowed to take individual examinations/courses and must comply with the curriuclum's registration requirements. Furthermore, you are not allowed to take any public defenses. Graduating is not possible.
- There might be restrictions/limitations when it comes to health insurance, residence permit and financial assistance. Please make an inquiry at the responsible authority about the consequences of your non-degree programme up front.

reranked.title: ABC of terminology
heading_path: T
reranked.text: in Vienna and Lower Austria](https://lehramt.univie.ac.at/) (in German) Related entries: - [Teacher Education for General Secondary Education in Vienna and Lower Austria](/en/abc-of-terminology/related-entries/?tx_irfaq_pi1%5BshowUid%5D=31331&cHash=a9508ca1d8a392e52ec541141e6afc7d) - Termination of a degree programme You can end your studies in the following ways: - successful graduation: You will receive your graduation documents in the StudyServiceCenter (SSC) of your degree programme (e.g. certificate, diploma supplement) - discontinuing your degree programme: You can print out a confirmation of withdrawal from university via u:space. Related Links: - [Closing degree programmes/withdrawal from University of Vienna](/en/admission/closing-a-degree-programme/) - [Graduations](https://studienpraeses.univie.ac.at/infos-zum-studienrecht/studienabschluesse/) Related entries: - [Completion of a degree programme](/en/abc-of-terminology/related-entries/?tx_irfaq_pi1%5BshowUid%5D=30172&cHash=3f411d6175185cfa1b401855807771dd) - [Closing a degree programme/withdrawing from the University of Vienna](/en/abc-of-terminology/related-entries/?tx_irfaq_pi1%5BshowUid%5D=31896&cHash=2e24f8e1dcdf22ae4e1123db52ef795d) - Tolerance semesters EU/EEA citizens and persons treated as equivalent only pay the Students' Union fee for the presecribed duration of study (= "minimum duration of study"). At the end of this period, they are granted two additional "tolerance semesters", during which only the Studens' Union fee is charged. If you do not complete your degree programme in the prescribed duration of studies + the two tolerance semesters, you will pay both the Students' Union fee and the tuition fee. Related Links: - [Tuition fee/Students' Union fee](/en/tuition-fee/) Related entries: - [Students’ Union fee (ÖH fee)](/en/abc-of-terminology/related-entries/?tx_irfaq_pi1%5BshowUid%5D=31205&cHash=ed830f954f65ec47794ec4809327b5d1) - [Tuition fee](/en/abc-of-terminology/related-entries/?tx_irfaq_pi1%5BshowUid%5D=31268&cHash=5186d8ac34fa30c4b1270fc725ce2ef7) - Transcript of records ("Sammelzeugnis") Your transcript of records lists all grades, positive and negative ones. It is an official proof of your academic progress, which you need

selected.title: Change of degree programme
heading_path: General information regarding the change of a degree programme
selected.text:
- Number of changes of degree programme: You can change your degree programme as often as you want to, according to the University of Vienna. However, a change of degree programme might impact your family allowance and/or study grant. Please contact the responsible authority (local Tax Office or Austrian Study Grant Authority) if you have any questions in this regard. Clarify the consequences on a potential residence permit with the Immigration Office (MA 35) or the responsible district commission ("Bezirkshauptmannschaft").
- Minimum academic achievement: Since winter semester 2022/23 onwards you have to achieve [at least 16 ECTS](/en/minimum-number-of-credits/) for each bachelor/diploma programme you are admitted to. If this is not the case, the degree programme will be closed.
- Transferring to the University of Vienna from another university: The information regarding the change of degree programmes applies to students admitted at the University of Vienna. Students who wish to transfer to the University of Vienna have to complete the admission procedure. The entry into a higher semester is not possible. After [completing the admission procedure](/en/admission-procedure/) you can apply for potential recognition of your previous examinations at the [StudiesServiceCenter or StudiesServiceUnit](https://ufind.univie.ac.at/en/search.html?filter=all&query=StudienServiceCenter) responsible for your degree programme. Thereby you might shorten the duration of your degree programme.

selected.title: Studying at the University of Vienna again – different degree programme
selected.text: You were admitted to a bachelor/diploma or master programme at the University of Vienna and want to study a different degree programme? Here you can find detailed information to best prepare your return to the University of Vienna.

selected.title: Studying at the University of Vienna again – different degree programme
heading_path: General information about your return to the University of Vienna
selected.text: - Minimum academic achievement: Since winter semester 2022/23 you have to achieve [at least 16 ECTS](/en/minimum-number-of-credits/) for each bachelor/diploma programme you are admitted to. If this is not the case, the degree programme will be closed. - Change of name: If your legal name has changed, please disclose this at an early stage. Message us via the [contact form](/en/contact-form/) and attach a scan of your valid passport or personal ID card and a proof of the name change (e.g. marriage certificate or official notice of the name change). - Application and admission period: The deadlines for your admission at the University of Vienna depend on the type of degree programme and your citizenship. You can find detailed information at the respective type of degree programme. - Recognition of exams: The [StudiesServiceCenter (SSC)/StudiesServiceUnit (SSS)](https://www.univie.ac.at/ueber-uns/leitung-organisation/studien-lehrorganisation/) of the respective degree programme can provide information regarding recognition of prior study achievements and help you to organise your studies. - Completion of the admission: You do not have to book an online appointment. - Simultaneous admission at two Austrian universities: It is not permitted to be admitted to the same degree programme at two Austrian universities at the same time. If you have already studied the chosen degree programme at another university, please send your confirmation of withdrawal via the [contact form](/en/contact-form/). - Organising your studies: [Tips and tricks to plan your studies and register

prompt_messages.content:
You are a concise assistant for prospective and current University of Vienna students. Answer the question using ONLY the numbered sources provided.
Rules:
- Base every statement on the sources; never add outside knowledge or guess.
- Cite each claim inline with the bracket number(s) of the source(s) you used, e.g. [1] or [2][3]. Cite only sources you actually relied on.
- If the sources do not contain the answer, reply that you don't know, and do NOT cite any sources.
- Keep the answer short and direct (it may be read aloud by a voice assistant).

Sources:
[1] Change of degree programme — General information regarding the change of a degree programme
- Number of changes of degree programme: You can change your degree programme as often as you want to, according to the University of Vienna. However, a change of degree programme might impact your family allowance and/or study grant. Please contact the responsible authority (local Tax Office or Austrian Study Grant Authority) if you have any questions in this regard. Clarify the consequences on a potential residence permit with the Immigration Office (MA 35) or the responsible district commission ("Bezirkshauptmannschaft").
- Minimum academic achievement: Since winter semester 2022/23 onwards you have to achieve [at least 16 ECTS](/en/minimum-number-of-credits/) for each bachelor/diploma programme you are admitted to. If this is not the case, the degree programme will be closed.
- Transferring to the University of Vienna from another university: The information regarding the change of degree programmes applies to students admitted at the University of Vienna. Students who wish to transfer to the University of Vienna have to complete the admission procedure. The entry into a higher semester is not possible. After [completing the admission procedure](/en/admission-procedure/) you can apply for potential recognition of your previous examinations at the [StudiesServiceCenter or StudiesServiceUnit](https://ufind.univie.ac.at/en/search.html?filter=all&query=StudienServiceCenter) responsible for your degree programme. Thereby you might shorten the duration of your degree programme.

[2] Exemptions from the entrance exam procedure — Changing your degree programme or studying an additional programme within certain groups > Studiengruppen
Collapse all Open all #### Business Administration ← → International Business Administration You are admitted to one of these two bachelor's programmes at the University of Vienna and want to change to the other programme or study both? Note the [deadline](#c4319) and message us. - Changing your degree programme: Define which programme you want to close and which you want to study in the future in the [Servicedesk](https://servicedesk.univie.ac.at/plugins/servlet/desk/portal/62/create/936). - Additional degree programme: Message us via the [Servicedesk](https://servicedesk.univie.ac.at/plugins/servlet/desk/portal/62/create/933) and tell us which programme you would like to study. #### Computer Science ← → Business Informatics You are admitted to one of these two bachelor's programmes at the University of Vienna and want to change to the other programme or study both? Note the [deadline](#c4319) and message us. - Changing your degree programme: Define which programme you want to close and which you want to study in the future in the [Servicedesk](https://servicedesk.univie.ac.at/plugins/servlet/desk/portal/62/create/936). - Additional degree programme: Message us via the [Servicedesk](https://servicedesk.univie.ac.at/plugins/servlet/desk/portal/62/create/933) and tell us which programme you would like to study. #### Political Science ← → Sociology ← → Cultural- and Social Anthropology You are admitted to one of these three bachelor's programmes at the University of Vienna? You can change between these three programmes or study two or all three programmes. Note the [deadline](#c4319) and message us. - Changing your degree programme: Define which programme you want to close and which

[3] Change of degree programme
You are currently studying at the University of Vienna and would like to change your degree programme? Your steps in the admission procedure and the application period depend on the type of degree programme.

[## Bachelor/diploma programmes](/en/admission/change-of-degree-programme/change-of-bachelordiploma-programme/)

[## Master programmes](/en/admission/change-of-degree-programme/change-of-master-programme/)

[4] Exemptions from the entrance exam procedure — Changing your degree programme or studying an additional programme within certain groups > Studiengruppen
programmes at the University of Vienna? You can change between these three programmes or study two or all three programmes. Note the [deadline](#c4319) and message us. - Changing your degree programme: Define which programme you want to close and which you want to study in the future in the [Servicedesk](https://servicedesk.univie.ac.at/plugins/servlet/desk/portal/62/create/936). - Additional degree programme: Message us via the [Servicedesk](https://servicedesk.univie.ac.at/plugins/servlet/desk/portal/62/create/933) and tell us which programme you would like to study.

[5] Studying at the University of Vienna again – different degree programme
You were admitted to a bachelor/diploma or master programme at the University of Vienna and want to study a different degree programme? Here you can find detailed information to best prepare your return to the University of Vienna.

[6] Studying at the University of Vienna again – different degree programme — General information about your return to the University of Vienna
- Minimum academic achievement: Since winter semester 2022/23 you have to achieve [at least 16 ECTS](/en/minimum-number-of-credits/) for each bachelor/diploma programme you are admitted to. If this is not the case, the degree programme will be closed. - Change of name: If your legal name has changed, please disclose this at an early stage. Message us via the [contact form](/en/contact-form/) and attach a scan of your valid passport or personal ID card and a proof of the name change (e.g. marriage certificate or official notice of the name change). - Application and admission period: The deadlines for your admission at the University of Vienna depend on the type of degree programme and your citizenship. You can find detailed information at the respective type of degree programme. - Recognition of exams: The [StudiesServiceCenter (SSC)/StudiesServiceUnit (SSS)](https://www.univie.ac.at/ueber-uns/leitung-organisation/studien-lehrorganisation/) of the respective degree programme can provide information regarding recognition of prior study achievements and help you to organise your studies. - Completion of the admission: You do not have to book an online appointment. - Simultaneous admission at two Austrian universities: It is not permitted to be admitted to the same degree programme at two Austrian universities at the same time. If you have already studied the chosen degree programme at another university, please send your confirmation of withdrawal via the [contact form](/en/contact-form/). - Organising your studies: [Tips and tricks to plan your studies and register

Question: How does changing to another bachelor programme work at the University of Vienna?

generate.raw_response: To change to another bachelor programme at the University of Vienna, you can change your degree programme as often as you want [1]. However, you must achieve at least 16 ECTS for each bachelor/diploma programme you are admitted to, or the degree programme will be closed [1][6]. You should define which programme you want to close and which you want to study in the future in the Servicedesk [2][4]. Additionally, exemptions from the entrance exam procedure may apply if you are changing between certain programmes, such as Business Administration and International Business Administration, or Computer Science and Business Informatics [2][4].

answer: To change to another bachelor programme at the University of Vienna, you can change your degree programme as often as you want [1]. However, you must achieve at least 16 ECTS for each bachelor/diploma programme you are admitted to, or the degree programme will be closed [1][6]. You should define which programme you want to close and which you want to study in the future in the Servicedesk [2][4]. Additionally, exemptions from the entrance exam procedure may apply if you are changing between certain programmes, such as Business Administration and International Business Administration, or Computer Science and Business Informatics [2][4].
